title: dactylis glomerata var. aschersoniana
intro: Dactylis glomerata var. aschersoniana, also known as orchard grass or cock's-foot, is a perennial grass species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata var. aschersoniana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and aid in the treatment of various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
